mapping_metadata.analyzer: Senior Test Automation Mapping Specialist Agent

test_case_mappings.test_case_description: Test case to verify the Billing Cycle Period card display and behavior on Print and Payment History page when a subscription plan is paused, including verification of complimentary pages progress bar, additional pages tracking, tooltips, and page usage information display across different printing scenarios.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Enroll the Ink printer with 50 pages plan or higher.
precondition_mapping.notes: This precondition requires printer enrollment methods or API helpers. Batch 6 contains only core infrastructure (browser manager, reporting, scraper, settings). Printer enrollment methods likely exist in helper or API utility modules in other batches.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the Subscription has no free months info.
precondition_mapping.notes: This precondition requires subscription verification methods or API helpers. Not found in this core infrastructure batch.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the subscription is "subscribed" status.
precondition_mapping.notes: This precondition requires subscription status verification methods or API helpers. Not found in this core infrastructure batch.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Click the Pause Plan button on CIT modal or "Pause Instant Ink" link to trigger the pause plan.

components_found_in_this_batch.what_it_does: Manages the complete lifecycle of Playwright browser automation including browser launch, context creation, page initialization, and cleanup. This is the foundational class that provides the browser and page objects needed to perform any UI interactions including clicking buttons and links.
components_found_in_this_batch.usage_example:
with PlaywrightManager() as page:
    # page object is now available for interactions
    page.goto('https://app.hpinstantink.com')
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: This precondition requires clicking UI elements (Pause Plan button or Pause Instant Ink link). PlaywrightManager provides the browser and page setup needed to perform these clicks. While it doesn't provide the specific page objects or locators for these elements, it provides the foundational browser infrastructure that all UI interactions depend on.

components_found_in_this_batch.what_it_does: Performs complete Playwright browser setup and returns a ready-to-use Page object. Starts Playwright, determines browser type based on target platform, launches browser with configured settings (headless mode, viewport, locale, HAR logging, video recording, proxy), creates browser context, and creates a new page. Returns the page object for use in test flows.
components_found_in_this_batch.usage_example:
manager = PlaywrightManager()
page = manager.setup()
# page is now ready for navigation and interactions
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: This method provides the Page object that is required to perform the click actions described in this precondition. It's the setup method that must be called before any UI interactions can occur.

components_found_in_this_batch.what_it_does: Performs complete cleanup of all Playwright resources in the correct order: page, context, browser, and playwright instance. Verifies that HAR and video files were saved if logging is enabled. All exceptions during cleanup are caught and logged to prevent cleanup failures from crashing the test.
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: This method is needed for test teardown after the precondition setup is complete. It ensures proper cleanup of browser resources.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Performs complete Playwright browser setup and returns a ready-to-use Page object. Configures browser type (chromium/firefox/webkit), viewport dimensions, locale, HAR logging, video recording, and proxy settings based on GlobalState configuration.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Provides the browser and page infrastructure needed to perform UI interactions for precondition 3 (clicking pause plan button/link). This is foundational setup required before any test steps can execute.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Performs complete cleanup of all Playwright resources including page, context, browser, and playwright instance. Verifies HAR and video files were saved if logging is enabled.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Provides cleanup functionality needed after test execution completes. This is part of the overall test infrastructure for proper resource management.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Initializes the framework configuration by resetting GlobalState and populating it with provided values and loaded data from locale_map.json, address_data.json, and printer_profiles.json. Sets up locale, language, region, country, printer profile, and other runtime configuration.
matched_methods.usage_example: Config.init(stack='prod', locale='US', language='en', headless=True, mode='validate', flow_name='instant_ink_billing')
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Provides configuration initialization needed before test execution. This sets up environment-specific settings, locale information, and printer profiles that are required for the test preconditions.

matched_helpers.what_it_does: Manages the complete lifecycle of Playwright browser automation including browser launch, context creation, page initialization, and cleanup. Implements context manager protocol for automatic resource management.
matched_helpers.usage_example:
with PlaywrightManager() as page:
    page.goto('https://app.hpinstantink.com')
    # perform test actions
matched_helpers.relevance_reason: Provides the browser infrastructure needed for all UI interactions in the test. This is the foundational helper that enables navigation, clicking, and all other browser-based actions.

matched_helpers.what_it_does: Configuration initialization class that populates GlobalState from command-line arguments and loads supporting data from JSON files including locale information, address data, and printer profiles.
matched_helpers.relevance_reason: Provides configuration management needed to set up test environment, locale settings, and printer profiles required by the test preconditions.

matched_constants.relevance_reason: Provides the default timeout value (30 seconds) that should be used for wait operations throughout the test. Many steps involve waiting for elements to be visible or for page loads to complete.

Provides the maximum retry count for operations that may fail transiently (page loads, API calls, element interactions). Useful for implementing retry logic in steps that involve navigation, time shifting, or print simulation.

step_level_mapping.step_action: Go to Print and Payment History page under HP Instant Ink.

action_components_found_in_this_batch.notes: Navigation to Print and Payment History page requires page objects (InstantInkDashboardPage or PrintAndPaymentHistoryPage) with navigation methods and locators for the navigation link. These components are not in this core infrastructure batch.

verification_components_found_in_this_ba.notes: Page load verification requires page object methods like verify_page_loaded() or is_page_visible(). These are not in this core infrastructure batch.

test_data_components_found_in_this_batch.notes: DEFAULT_TIMEOUT constant is useful for page load waits. The actual URL for the Print and Payment History page is not in this batch.

test_case_mappings.batch_analysis_summary: Batch 6 contains core infrastructure modules for the AURA framework: PlaywrightManager for browser lifecycle management, ReportsManager for test execution reporting, scraper for UI element extraction and baseline creation, and settings for configuration management. This batch provides foundational infrastructure components but does NOT contain the page objects, locators, action methods, or verification methods specific to HP Instant Ink pages that are required by test case C44873414. The test case requires PrintAndPaymentHistoryPage and InstantInkDashboardPage page objects with their associated locators and methods for interacting with billing cycle cards, progress bars, tooltips, and info icons. It also requires API helpers for time manipulation and print job simulation. These components are not in this core infrastructure batch and likely exist in other batches containing page objects and helper modules. The only relevant components found in this batch are: (1) PlaywrightManager for browser setup/teardown infrastructure, (2) Config/GlobalState for configuration management, and (3) Constants for timeout and retry values. These are useful for test infrastructure and configuration but do not directly implement the test case logic.
